    Research

    DoYun Park

    • South Korea
    • Email

    A wireless mask-free capnography patch for ambulatory end-tidal CO2 and cardiopulmonary monitoring.”

    • Yoojin Kang*, Do Yun Park*, Youngmin Sim, Gyurim Jang, Jun Yeong Kim, Kyeongha Kwon
    • * These authors contributed equally
    Capnography provides critical respiratory information through end-tidal CO2 (EtCO2), which is the peak CO2 concentration at the end of the exhalation. However, conventional systems require stationary monitors, bulky tubing, and wired connections, limiting their use outside clinical settings. Here, we report a wireless, mask-free capnography system worn beneath the nose. The system enables real-time EtCO2 and respiratory rate monitoring and communicates with a smartphone via Bluetooth Low Energy for real-time display and logging. The compact, mask-free design and wireless connectivity expand the potential applications of conventional capnography beyond clinical environments, including chronic disease management and home-based sleep studies.
